h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: 0.04% still feels like thin arithmetic to hang climate-scale claims on, even with that meter blinking at 420
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Fine, read it as blockers stacked through the column instead of a spoonful lost in air.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turns were heading toward the learner pressing for a quantitative justification of the valve/leverage claim within the same percentage-of-air framing.

scored.recoheredEarlier: If the words are exact, it seems fair, or at least that was the standard in my head.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: the earlier mistake was letting clean attribution excuse a crop that makes the source sound more absolute
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was moving toward a practical procedural question about where to draw the tightening-versus-steering line at lock time, implying the exactness-as-fairness standard was already partially corrected and the remaining puzzle was only operational.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought the wall map was the thing to trust because Greenland looks almost absurdly large up there.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: It still jars visually, but I should mark projection distortion, not Greenland nearly Africa-sized.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turns were heading toward a simple correction loop in which the learner progressively accepts the area figures and abandons the wall-map impression.

scored.recoheredEarlier: My first instinct is still to say the average tells us what this person probably got
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turns were heading toward a progressively refined but still forward-moving negotiation of which hedge word ('resembles,' 'consistent with') is safe enough to use.

scored.recoheredEarlier: Okay, the steel is not the bridge; the route is the bridge, and a loose gusset can steal the whole load before the member even gets to prove itself.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: New version: the clamp has to show whether the load actually passes from nose to deck to diagonal to gusset to support, or leaks out as slip, rotation, or a bowed compression bar.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turns were heading toward incremental refinement of the route-is-bridge principle, with the learner dutifully narrowing the claim while still treating the original formulation as sound.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought the person's body gets used to the antibiotic, and that made the patient the changing thing on my poster.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: That helps, but I still need to keep straight: bacteria change; the patient's body does not become antibiotic-resistant.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turns were heading toward simple factual correction and consolidation of the right sentence for the poster, with the learner progressively accepting the bacteria-as-agent framing.
